name: VLM Computer Use Coding Agent

personas.name: IDE Coder
personas.description: IDE Coder operates VS Code entirely via GUI - creating files, writing Python code, using IntelliSense, navigating with keyboard shortcuts, and saving work.

personas.name: Terminal Operator
personas.description: Terminal Operator executes commands in VS Code integrated terminal - running Python scripts, pip install, pytest, git commands, and reading output.

personas.name: File Manager
personas.description: File Manager creates project structure via File Explorer and VS Code - making folders (src, tests, docs), organizing files, and managing workspace.

personas.name: Debugger
personas.description: Debugger uses VS Code debugging GUI - setting breakpoints by clicking line numbers, stepping through code, inspecting variables, and fixing issues.

personas.name: Browser Researcher
personas.description: Browser Researcher opens Chrome to search documentation, Stack Overflow, and GitHub - copying code snippets and pasting into the IDE.

actions: Open VS Code via Start menu or desktop shortcut
Click File > Open Folder and create new project folder called MyPythonProject in Documents
In VS Code Explorer, right-click and create src folder
Right-click and create tests folder
Right-click and create docs folder
Open integrated terminal using Ctrl+` or View > Terminal
In terminal, type 'python -m venv venv' and press Enter to create virtual environment
In terminal, type 'git init' and press Enter to initialize repository
flows.sub_goal: Set up complete Python project structure with folders, virtual environment, and git

actions: Right-click on src folder in Explorer and select New File, name it main.py
Click into editor and type the Python shebang and docstring at top of file
Type import statements: import sys, import argparse
Type main function with argparse setup and basic logic
Type if __name__ == '__main__': sys.exit(main()) at bottom
Press Ctrl+S to save the file
Right-click src folder, create utils.py with utility functions
Right-click src folder, create __init__.py to make it a package
Save all files with Ctrl+S
flows.sub_goal: Write modular Python code with main entry point and utility modules

actions: Right-click on tests folder and create test_main.py
Type import pytest and from src import main at top
Write test functions using def test_function_name(): pattern
Add assertions using assert statements
Save the test file with Ctrl+S
Right-click tests folder, create __init__.py
Create conftest.py with any shared fixtures
flows.sub_goal: Create pytest test files covering main functionality

actions: Click into the integrated terminal
Type '.\venv\Scripts\activate' and press Enter to activate venv
Type 'pip install pytest pytest-cov' and press Enter
Type 'pytest tests/ -v' and press Enter to run tests
Read the test output to see passed/failed tests
If tests fail, note the error messages for debugging
flows.sub_goal: Run pytest and verify all tests pass

actions: Open the Python file to debug in VS Code editor
Click on line number where you want breakpoint - red dot appears
Press F5 or click Run and Debug icon in activity bar
Select 'Python File' from debug configuration dropdown
When breakpoint hits, use F10 to step over, F11 to step into
Hover over variables to see values in tooltip
Check Variables pane in Debug sidebar for current state
Press F5 to continue or Shift+F5 to stop debugging
flows.sub_goal: Debug Python code using VS Code visual debugger with breakpoints

actions: Right-click project root in Explorer, create README.md
Type markdown header: # MyPythonProject
Add description section explaining what the project does
Add installation section with pip install instructions
Add usage section with code examples
Add testing section explaining how to run tests
Save with Ctrl+S
Create requirements.txt with project dependencies
flows.sub_goal: Create comprehensive README and requirements documentation

actions: Click Source Control icon in VS Code activity bar (branch icon)
Review changed files listed in the panel
Click + icon next to each file to stage, or click + next to Changes to stage all
Type commit message in the text box at top
Click checkmark icon or press Ctrl+Enter to commit
Alternatively in terminal: type 'git add .' and Enter
Type 'git commit -m "Initial commit"' and Enter
flows.sub_goal: Stage and commit changes using Git via VS Code GUI or terminal

goal: Develop Python applications entirely through VS Code GUI interaction - creating projects, writing code, running tests, debugging, and managing git - all via visual computer use without direct file system access.
